subject: Geography
title: Greenland: Island or Continent?
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Greenland: Island or Continent?.

hook.text: Greenland is the world's largest island—yet it's three times smaller than Australia.

buildup.text: Its ice sheet covers 80% of the surface and holds enough water to raise seas by seven meters.

discovery.text: Vikings named it 'Greenland' to attract settlers—a bit of medieval marketing.

twist.text: Under the ice, scientists found a valley bigger than the Grand Canyon.

climax.text: Greenland's ice is melting faster than any model predicted, redrawing coastlines worldwide.

punchline.text: The world's biggest island is mostly ice—for now.

quiz.question: Why did Vikings name the island 'Greenland'?
options: To attract settlers with a pleasant name
It was covered in green vegetation
